segments.speaker: son
segments.text: Dad, if Arjuna was such a great warrior, why did he suddenly become confused on the battlefield?

segments.speaker: father
segments.text: Because in that moment, he was not seeing enemies. He was seeing his own people, his teachers, and his relatives.

segments.speaker: daughter
segments.text: So it was not weakness exactly. It was more like his emotions became too heavy?

segments.speaker: mother
segments.text: Yes. Sometimes good people feel confused when duty and emotion pull them in two different directions.

segments.speaker: father
segments.text: Imagine a symbolic view of Kurukshetra behind him. The outer battle was real, but the inner battle inside his mind was even stronger.

segments.speaker: son
segments.text: Oh. So Krishna first helped him clear his mind before asking him to act?
